raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2177 would enact comprehensive reforms and improvements to horse racing to eliminate serious contributing factors to equine deaths.",
    "The overall horse racing industry has consistently sustained more than 100 horse deaths every year on all California tracks combined.",
    "Evidence from necropsies of horses that suffer catastrophic injuries shows that the vast majority of racehorses had pre-existing injuries that went unnoticed.",
    "The misuse of medication and the lack of procedures and technology to identify injuries have become factors in horse deaths.",
    "AB 2177 mandates the use of CT scan equipment for screening horses.",
    "AB 2177 requires an onsite central pharmacy at major tracks to control medication use and prevent abuse.",
    "AB 2177 prohibits veterinarians from prescribing medications for anything other than the diagnosed condition.",
    "The California Horse Racing Board can suspend or revoke a trainer’s license for repeat violations of medication regulations.",
    "Decades of necropsies on thousands of horses who broke bones on California tracks indicate that these animals need fewer medications."
  ]
}
```
